buttons.base: inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:opacity-50 disabled:pointer-events-none ring-offset-background

inputs.base: flex h-12 w-full rounded-md border-2 border-gray-400 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-base ring-offset-background file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-gray-500 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[#2e5496] focus-visible:border-[#2e5496] disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

cards.base: rounded-lg border border-gray-200 bg-white text-gray-950 shadow-sm
cards.header: flex flex-col space-y-1.5 p-6 border-b border-gray-100

sidebar.container: h-screen w-64 bg-[#2e5496] text-white flex flex-col fixed left-0 top-0 z-50
sidebar.link_base: flex items-center gap-3 px-6 py-4 text-sm font-medium transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 w-full
sidebar.header: h-16 flex items-center px-6 font-bold text-xl tracking-wider text-white border-b border-white/10

login_background.usage: Full screen background with overlay on Login page

logo.description: Gold Star Icon + Bold White Text

accessibility_rules.focus_states: All interactive elements MUST have a visible focus ring (2px solid #FFD700 or #2e5496).
accessibility_rules.contrast: Text must maintain 4.5:1 ratio (AA) or 7:1 (AAA). Use the provided color palette.
accessibility_rules.semantic_html: Use <main>, <nav>, <aside>, <header>, <footer>. Use <button> for actions, <a> for links.
accessibility_rules.forms: All inputs must have associated <label> elements. No placeholders as labels.
accessibility_rules.aria: Use aria-label for icon-only buttons. Use aria-live for status updates.

general: Follow the 'E1' persona: clean, structured, no clutter.
Strictly enforce the Blue (#2e5496) and Gold (#FFD700) theme.
Ensure the 'View/Print' page matches the 'original' requirement: strict grid, black and white, suitable for printing on paper.
Do not use gradients for the dashboard background; keep it clean white/gray.

pages.login: Centered card on backdrop image. High contrast inputs.
pages.dashboard: Data table with specific status colors. Search bar prominent.
pages.intake_form: Grid layout. 'Set to Today' buttons next to date inputs. No dropdowns - text inputs only.
pages.print_view: Minimalist. No sidebar. High density for printing.

tech_stack.frontend: React (JS), Tailwind, Shadcn UI, Axios, React Router

universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
